description: city in Tottori prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Yonago (米子市, Yonago-shi) is a city in western Tottori Prefecture, Japan.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Tottori Prefecture (鳥取県, Tottori-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [tot.to.ɾʲi, tot.to.ɾʲiꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

description: census-designated place in San Joaquin County, California, United States
description_wiki: Linden is a census-designated place (CDP) in San Joaquin County, California, United States.

description: county seat of San Joaquin County, California, United States
description_wiki: Stockton is a city in and the county seat of San Joaquin County, California, United States.

description: 1894–1895 war between the Qing dynasty and the Empire of Japan over influence in Joseon, fought chiefly in Joseon
description_wiki: The First Sino-Japanese War (25 July 1894 – 17 April 1895), or the First China–Japan War, was a conflict between the Qing dynasty of China and the Empire of Japan primarily for influence over Korea.

description: provincial-level municipality in China
description_wiki: Shanghai (; Chinese: 上海; pinyin: Shànghǎi, Shanghainese: zaon6 he5 [zɑ̃˩ hɛ˦] , Standard Chinese pronunciation: [ʂâŋ.xàɪ] ) is a direct-administered municipality and the most populous urban area in China.

description: city and special administrative region of China
description_wiki: Hong Kong is a special administrative region of China.

aliases: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
description: island country in north-west Europe
description_wiki: The United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, commonly known as the United Kingdom (UK) or Britain, is a country in northwestern Europe, off the coast of the continental mainland.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: country in Eastern Europe and Northern Asia
description_wiki: Russia, or the Russian Federation, is a country in Eastern Europe and North Asia.

description: conflict between the Russian and Japanese empires from 1904 to 1905
description_wiki: The Russo-Japanese War (8 February 1904 – 5 September 1905) was fought between the Russian Empire and the Empire of Japan over rival imperial ambitions in Manchuria and the Korean Empire.

description: regional command of the Russian (and formerly Soviet) Navy
description_wiki: The Baltic Fleet (Russian: Балтийский флот, romanized: Baltiyskiy flot) is the fleet of the Russian Navy in the Baltic Sea.

description: sovereign island country and city-state in maritime Southeast Asia
description_wiki: Singapore, officially the Republic of Singapore, is an island country and city-state in Southeast Asia.

description: marginal sea between Japan, Russia, and Korea
description_wiki: The Sea of Japan (see below for other names) is the marginal sea between the Japanese archipelago, Sakhalin, the Korean Peninsula, and the mainland of the Russian Far East.

description: naval branch of the Empire of Japan
description_wiki:
The Imperial Japanese Navy (IJN) was the navy of the Empire of Japan from 1868 to 1945, when it was dissolved following Japan's surrender in World War II. The Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF) was formed between 1952 and 1954 after the dissolution of the IJN.
The IJN was the third largest navy in the world by 1920, behind the Royal Navy and the United States Navy (USN).

description: Japanese admiral of the fleet (1848–1934)
description_wiki: Tōgō Heihachirō (東郷 平八郎; 27 January 1848 – 30 May 1934), served as a gensui or admiral of the fleet in the Imperial Japanese Navy and became one of Japan's greatest naval heroes.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: mountain in Tokyo, Japan
description_wiki: Mount Takao (高尾山, Takao-san) is a mountain in the city of Hachiōji, Tokyo, Japan.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Shimane Prefecture (島根県, Shimane-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [ɕiꜜ.ma.ne, ɕi.ma.neꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

description: 1900 pre-dreadnought battleship
description_wiki: Mikasa (三笠) is a pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Japanese Navy (IJN) in the late 1890s, and is the only ship of her class.

description: 1899 Shikishima-class battleship
description_wiki: Hatsuse (初瀬, Hatsuse) was a Shikishima-class pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Japanese Navy (IJN) in the late 1890s.

description: third largest island of Japan
second largest and populated city in Osaka Prefecture, Japan
global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: dynamite explosion on railway line near Mukden (Shenyang) in 1931
geographic region in Northeast Asia
description_wiki: Manchuria is a historical region in Northeast Asia encompassing the entirety of present-day Northeast China and parts of the modern-day Russian Far East south of the Uda River and the Tukuringra-Dzhagdy ranges.

description: international humanitarian movement
description_wiki: The International Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement is a humanitarian movement with approximately 16 million volunteers, members, and staff worldwide.

description: school of Mahāyāna Buddhist Tradition
description_wiki: Zen (Japanese pronunciation: [dzeꜜɴ, dzeɴ]; from Chinese: Chan; in Korean: Sŏn, and Vietnamese: Thiền) is a Mahayana Buddhist tradition that developed in China during the Tang dynasty by blending Indian Mahayana Buddhism, particularly Yogacara and Madhyamaka philosophies, with Chinese Taoist thought, especially Neo-Daoist.

description: a polytheistic and animistic religion originating in Japan
description_wiki: Shinto (神道, Shintō; Japanese pronunciation: [ɕiꜜn.toː]), also called Shintoism, is the native religion and former ethnic cult of Japan.

description: Japanese holiday to celebrate the foundation of Japan.
description_wiki: National Foundation Day (建国記念の日, Kenkoku Kinen no Hi) is a public holiday of Japan observed annually on the 11th of February.

description: Abrahamic monotheistic religion founded on the teachings, person and life of Jesus
description_wiki: Christianity is an Abrahamic monotheistic religion, which states that Jesus is the Son of God and rose from the dead after his crucifixion, whose coming as the messiah (Christ) was prophesied in the Old Testament and chronicled in the New Testament.

description: city in Shimane prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Matsue (Japanese: 松江市, Hepburn: Matsue-shi) is the capital city of Shimane Prefecture, Japan, located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Welkin may refer to:

description: one of the four canonical gospels in the New Testament
description_wiki: The Gospel of John is the fourth of the New Testament's canonical Gospels.

description: capital city of Hyogo Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Kobe ( KOH-bay; Japanese: 神戸, romanized: Kōbe, pronounced [koꜜː.be] ), officially Kobe City (神戸市, Kōbe-shi; [koː.beꜜ.ɕi]), is the capital city of Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan.

description: capital city of the province of British Columbia, Canada
description_wiki: Victoria is the capital city of the Canadian province of British Columbia, located on the southern tip of Vancouver Island off Canada's Pacific coast.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

aliases: Great San Francisco Earthquake and Fire
description: major earthquake that struck San Francisco and the coast of Northern California
description_wiki: The 1906 San Francisco earthquake was a major earthquake which struck the coast of Northern California at 05:12 am Pacific Standard Time on Wednesday, April 18, 1906.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: Wikimedia set index article
description_wiki: Tenyo Maru is the name of the following ships:

description: city in Shimane prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Matsue (Japanese: 松江市, Hepburn: Matsue-shi) is the capital city of Shimane Prefecture, Japan, located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

surface_forms: Gentleman's Agreement between Japan and the U.S.
Alien Land bill in 1920 in California

description: organization
description_wiki: The Japanese American Citizens League (日系アメリカ人市民同盟, Nikkei Amerikajin Shimin Dōmei) is an Asian American civil rights charity, headquartered in San Francisco, with regional chapters across the United States.

description: World War II Japanese-American internment camp in California
description_wiki: Manzanar is the site of one of ten American concentration camps, where more than 120,000 Japanese Americans were incarcerated during World War II, from March 1942 to November 1945.
